rubric_results.check: Title is non-empty and plausibly the document's title?
rubric_results.note: The title references a specific named reservation and document type (EIS), which is plausible for an Environmental Impact Statement.

rubric_results.check: Length is reasonable (not a sentence fragment, not the whole abstract)?
rubric_results.note: Title is concise and appropriately scoped for a document title.

rubric_results.check: If two sources (NUL + Haiku) — do they roughly agree?
rubric_results.note: Only one source (NUL) was provided; no Haiku source to compare.

title.notes: The extracted title is plausible and well-formed for an EIS document concerning the Havasupai Indian Reservation. However, no cited page text was provided to verify the extraction, and only a single source (NUL) was used. Confidence is medium due to the absence of corroborating page-level evidence.

rubric_results.check: Year falls within 1969 ≤ year ≤ current year?
rubric_results.note: 2026 is a future year beyond the current year (2025), failing the upper bound check

rubric_results.check: Year present in NUL metadata OR found on first 3 pages?
rubric_results.note: NUL metadata reportedly yields 2026, though no cited page text is provided to verify; regex from first 3 pages yields 1981

rubric_results.check: If NUL and regex disagree, value reflects this with a low-confidence flag?
rubric_results.note: Extraction correctly flags the disagreement (NUL=2026 vs regex=1981) and assigns low confidence

year.notes: The extracted year of 2026 exceeds the current year and is therefore an invalid EIS publication date. The regex-derived value of 1981 is plausible for an EIS document and falls within the valid range. No cited page text was provided to adjudicate between the two sources. A human reviewer should inspect the document directly to confirm whether 1981 (regex) or another year is correct.

rubric_results.check: Type is one of allowed values
rubric_results.note: Draft is an allowed type, but evidence strongly suggests Final

rubric_results.check: Regex and Sonnet agree
rubric_results.note: Extraction note explicitly states regex=Draft disagrees with Sonnet=Final; they do not agree

rubric_results.check: Evidence phrase supports chosen type
rubric_results.note: Evidence phrase 'FINAL ENVIRONMENTAL STATEMENT - VOLUME II' directly contradicts the extracted value of Draft

eis_type.notes: The extracted value of 'Draft' is directly contradicted by the cited evidence phrase 'FINAL ENVIRONMENTAL STATEMENT - VOLUME II', which clearly indicates a Final EIS. The regex and Sonnet signals disagree, and the extractor's own confidence is low. The correct value should almost certainly be 'Final' based on the available evidence. Re-extraction with priority given to the literal evidence phrase is strongly recommended.

rubric_results.check: All listed agencies appear in NUL contributors OR on cited title pages?
rubric_results.note: United States. Bureau of Indian Affairs is sourced from NUL and confirmed present there.

rubric_results.check: Joint-lead (>2 agencies) flagged for human review?
rubric_results.note: Only one agency listed; joint-lead check not applicable.

rubric_results.check: No role text left in agency names?
rubric_results.note: The agency name contains no parenthetical role text or issuing body descriptors.

lead_agency.notes: A single lead agency, United States. Bureau of Indian Affairs, is extracted and confirmed via NUL. The name is clean with no extraneous role text. No cited title pages were provided, but NUL sourcing is sufficient for a passing verdict given high extraction confidence.

rubric_results.check: project_description text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: PL 93-620, 185,000 acres, 95,300 acres traditional use area, 20 divisions, and agriculture/grazing details are all visible in the cited pages. However, the specific '500-600 animal units,' 'seven units rotation grazing,' and '$1.855 million range program cost' are NOT visible in the provided page text.

rubric_results.check: numbers, places, dates match cited pages
rubric_results.note: PL 93-620 signed January 3, 1975 is confirmed (88 Stat. 2089 referenced). 185,000 acres and ~95,300 acres confirmed. However, '500-600 animal units,' 'seven units,' '$1.855 million,' '281 residents,' '452 enrolled members,' '115 off-reservation,' '500 acres at bottom of Havasu Canyon,' '$200,000 FY1976 tourism,' '10,000 annual visitors,' '120-180 horses' are not visible in the provided cited page text.

rubric_results.check: public_response limited to main doc (based_on_main_doc_only=true)
rubric_results.note: The public_response field references the draft EIS and public hearings described within the document itself; no external sources cited.

rubric_results.check: no invention or hallucinated detail beyond cited content
rubric_results.note: Multiple specific figures (500-600 animal units, $1.855 million, 281 residents, 452 enrolled members, 115 off-reservation, 500 acres confined area, $200,000 tourism revenue, 10,000 visitors, 120-180 horses) appear in the extraction but are not present in the provided cited page text, suggesting either hallucination or reliance on pages not shown.

summary.notes: The extraction contains many specific numeric details (population figures, livestock counts, cost estimates, tourism revenue) that do not appear in the provided cited page text. These figures may exist in the full document on pages not shown in the excerpt (e.g., socioeconomic sections), or they may be hallucinated. The public hearing details (300 copies, three hearings in September 1979 in Supai/Kingman/Flagstaff, agency responses) are confirmed by the cited pages. Human review is needed to verify the unconfirmed numeric claims against the full document.

rubric_results.check: Each listed alternative is actually discussed on its cited page?
rubric_results.note: Pages 62-64 are not present in the cited text. The text provided includes the cover/summary section and Table of Contents. 'No Action' appears only as a header in item 4 of the summary sheet, not as a substantive discussion on pages 62-64. 'Project Alternatives' is listed in the TOC as Chapter VIII (page VIII-1), not pages 62-64.

rubric_results.check: The No Action alternative is included if the cited text includes it?
rubric_results.note: No Action is mentioned in item 4 of the summary sheet, and the extraction includes it.

rubric_results.check: Names and descriptions match terminology used in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: 'No Action,' 'Proposed Action,' and 'Project Alternatives' all appear as terminology in the provided text (summary sheet and TOC).

rubric_results.check: No outside-knowledge alternatives added that aren't in the chapter?
rubric_results.note: The three alternatives named correspond to terms found in the provided text; no extraneous alternatives were introduced.

alternatives.notes: The cited source pages (62-64) do not appear in the provided text excerpt, which only contains the cover summary sheet and Table of Contents. The actual alternatives discussion is in Chapter VIII (Project Alternatives, page VIII-1), not pages 62-64. The descriptions of No Action and Proposed Action are drawn from the brief summary sheet rather than a dedicated alternatives section, and the 'Project Alternatives' entry explicitly acknowledges its details are not present. The extraction should be re-done against the actual Chapter VIII content.

rubric_results.check: Each chosen theme is supported by the doc summary fields?
rubric_results.note: No cited pages or summary text were provided, making it impossible to verify that the three themes are actually supported by the document content. The justification references specific details (Havasupai Tribe, bighorn sheep, Havasu Creek) but no source text is available to confirm.

rubric_results.check: Theme names come from the fixed taxonomy verbatim?
rubric_results.note: Cannot confirm against taxonomy without the taxonomy document, but the names 'Indigenous Narratives and Sovereignty,' 'Wildlife and Natural Areas,' and 'Water Systems' appear plausible as taxonomy entries. Flagged as n/a due to inability to verify verbatim match.

rubric_results.check: Subthemes belong to the chosen theme(s) per the taxonomy?
rubric_results.note: Subthemes appear logically aligned with the chosen themes (e.g., Sovereignty/Indigenous Knowledge under Indigenous Narratives; Habitat Conservation under Wildlife; Water Infrastructure/Scarcity under Water Systems), but cannot confirm taxonomy membership without the taxonomy document.

rubric_results.check: 1–3 themes and 2–5 subthemes selected (within range)?
rubric_results.note: 3 themes and 5 subthemes selected — both within the allowed ranges.

themes.notes: The extraction is internally coherent and the counts are within range, but no cited page text was provided, making it impossible to verify that the document summary actually supports these themes. The taxonomy verbatim match and subtheme assignments also cannot be confirmed without the taxonomy reference. A human reviewer should validate the source text and taxonomy alignment before accepting this extraction.

rubric_results.check: Place names appear in cited pages?
rubric_results.note: Havasupai Indian Reservation, Grand Canyon National Park, Havasu Canyon, and Pasture Wash all appear explicitly and repeatedly throughout the cited pages (1-30, which covers the full document text provided).

rubric_results.check: is_multi_site set correctly?
rubric_results.note: The EIS covers a single 185,000-acre addition to the Havasupai Indian Reservation; is_multi_site=false is appropriate. The various named features (Havasu Canyon, Pasture Wash) are sub-areas within this single reservation addition, not separate independent sites.

rubric_results.check: For corridor projects: endpoints named, not just a midpoint?
rubric_results.note: This is not a corridor project; it is a land use plan for a contiguous reservation addition.

rubric_results.check: State abbreviations match U.S. conventions?
rubric_results.note: All four places are assigned 'AZ' for Arizona, which is the correct USPS two-letter abbreviation and matches the document's repeated references to 'northern Arizona' and 'Arizona'.

rubric_results.check: Geocoded coordinates accurate?
rubric_results.note: The geocoded coordinates for 'Havasu Canyon' (lat 33.67, lon -112.43) resolve to Surprise, Maricopa County — a suburb of Phoenix — rather than the actual Havasu Canyon near the Grand Canyon (~36.2°N, ~112.7°W). This is a significant geocoding error likely caused by a name collision.

location.notes: All four place names are well-attested in the source pages, state abbreviations are correct, and is_multi_site is appropriately set to false for this single contiguous land addition. However, the geocoded coordinates for 'Havasu Canyon' are badly wrong — they point to a street address in Surprise, AZ (Maricopa County) rather than the actual Havasu Canyon in Coconino County near the Grand Canyon. The extracted textual location data is sound; only the geocoding for that one entry needs correction.

rubric_results.check: Each agency preparer named in the cited Preparers/Consultation text?
rubric_results.note: The cited pages (64-191) do not contain a Preparers/Consultation section in the provided text; the text covers Chapters I and II (description of proposed action and environment). No preparers are listed in the extracted text, and the extraction correctly leaves agency_preparers empty, but the source page reference is unverifiable from the provided text.

rubric_results.check: Cooperating agencies / tribal nations actually listed in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: The provided cited text does not contain a formal cooperating agencies list. However, several of the named entities (Bureau of Indian Affairs, National Park Service, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, Arizona State Game and Fish Department, Kaibab National Forest, Hualapai Indian Tribe, Havasupai Tribal Council) are mentioned throughout the body text in various capacities. They are not listed as 'cooperating agencies' in any formal section visible in the provided pages.

rubric_results.check: For public_commenters: stance present on cited page? Quote verbatim? Speaker clear?
rubric_results.note: No public commenters were extracted; field is empty.

rubric_results.check: Private individuals: last-name-only or 'private commenter' convention followed?
rubric_results.note: No private individuals were extracted as commenters.

key_people.notes: The cited source pages (64-191) do not appear in the provided text excerpt, which only covers early chapters of the EIS (Chapters I and II). The cooperating agencies listed in the extraction are mentioned in the body text but not in a formal cooperating agencies or consultation section visible here. The actual Consultation and Coordination section (Chapter IX, page IX-1) is referenced in the table of contents but not provided, making it impossible to verify whether these entities are formally listed as cooperating agencies. Human review is needed to confirm against the actual pages 64-191 and Chapter IX.
